title: 7 Herbal Teas to Help Pregnant Women Manage Chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been used for centuries to help with chills and body aches. This is due to its active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These compounds help to reduce the severity of chills and fever, making it easier to recover from illness.  To use ginger tea for chills, start by making a tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water. You can also add honey and lemon to taste. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate chills and body aches. Additionally, you can try taking a warm bath or shower after drinking the tea to help increase blood circulation and reduce chills.  When you take action, you can expect the chills to start subsiding within a few hours. Your body temperature should return to normal, and you may also start feeling more energetic and less tired. However, if your chills are severe, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been used for centuries to help with chills and colds. The reason they work is due to their high content of anti-inflammatory compounds like alkylamides and phenolic acids, which help reduce swelling and fight off infection. The tea also contains flavonoids and glycosides, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the body from oxidative stress. When you drink Echinacea purpurea tea, the active compounds are absorbed into your body, where they can help stimulate your immune system and increase the production of white blood cells. This helps your body fight off the underlying infection that's causing your chills. If you're experiencing chills, you can try drinking Echinacea purpurea tea by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried roots in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. It's best to drink the tea 2-3 times a day, and to continue drinking it for 3-5 days to see the full benefits. As your body starts to fight off the infection, you may begin to feel better, with your chills subsiding and your energy levels increasing. You may also notice a reduction in your fever and a decrease in the severity of your symptoms. However, it's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea tea is not a substitute for medical treatment, and you should consult with a healthcare professional if your symptoms persist or worsen.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used for centuries to help with chills and other cold-related issues. The key reason for this is the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are compounds found in the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the severity of chills and other symptoms. They also improve blood circulation, which can help to warm the body from the inside out. If you're experiencing chills, you can try drinking Ginkgo biloba tea to help alleviate your symptoms. To make the tea, steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in a cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the leaves and drink the tea as soon as it's cool enough to consume. You can drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, but make sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you're already taking any medications or have any underlying health conditions. When you drink Ginkgo biloba tea, you can expect to feel some relief from your chills within a few hours. The tea may help to reduce your body temperature, ease your muscle aches and pains, and improve your overall sense of well-being. However, it's essential to note that Ginkgo biloba tea may not cure chills, but it can help to manage your symptoms and make you feel more comfortable. As with any herbal remedy, it's crucial to use it responsibly and in moderation.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, is a natural remedy that can help alleviate chills. This is due to its high content of flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These compounds help to reduce fever and ease body aches associated with chills. Yarrow tea also contains essential oils like borneol and bornyl acetate, which can help to relax and soothe the body. If you're experiencing chills, consider making a tea from dried yarrow flowers. To do this, steep one teaspoon of dried yarrow in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. It's essential to note that yarrow tea is not a substitute for medical treatment and should not be taken by pregnant or breastfeeding women, as it may stimulate the uterus. Taking yarrow tea may lead to a reduction in fever and body aches, allowing you to feel more comfortable and relaxed. You may also notice an improvement in your sleep quality and a decrease in your overall sense of fatigue. However, if your chills are severe or accompanied by other symptoms like difficulty breathing or chest pain, seek medical attention immediately.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, can be a helpful remedy for chills. This is because the tea contains properties like rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. It also contains vitamins and minerals like vitamin C and potassium, which can help to regulate body temperature and boost the immune system. When you take Melissa officinalis teas for chills, try to drink at least 2-3 cups in a day, and make sure to sip it slowly and gently. You can add a bit of honey to make it more palatable, but avoid adding too much sugar. As you drink the tea, you may start to feel a warmth in your body, and your chills may begin to subside. The tea may also help to reduce fever and alleviate any discomfort or fatigue you're experiencing. It's best to drink the tea in the morning or early afternoon, when your body temperature is naturally lower, and avoid drinking it close to bedtime, as it may interfere with your sleep.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, also known as Ceylon cinnamon tea, can help alleviate chills due to its natural properties and constituents. The tea contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to reduce fever and soothe the body. Cinnamomum verum also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that can help boost the immune system and fight off underlying infections.  If you're experiencing chills, consider making a cup of Ceylon cinnamon tea by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of Cinnamomum verum powder in hot water for 5-7 minutes. You can add honey or lemon to taste. To enhance its effects, drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, and combine it with other remedies like staying hydrated, getting enough rest, and practicing good hygiene.  By taking these steps, you may experience a reduction in chills and fever, as well as an improvement in your overall energy levels. Your body's immune system may also become more resilient to infections, helping you recover faster from illness. However, it's essential to note that Cinnamomum verum tea is not a substitute for medical treatment. If your chills persist or worsen, consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and care.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal teas for chills

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas are a popular herbal remedy used to help alleviate chills. The tea's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce fever and soothe the body. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to the development of chills. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra teas a natural and effective way to help manage chills. If you're experiencing chills, try taking a warm bath or shower before drinking a cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea. Wrap yourself in a blanket and rest in a quiet, warm space to help your body recover. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, and consider adding other soothing herbs like peppermint or chamomile to enhance its calming effects. You may start to feel a reduction in chills and fever within a few hours, and your body should begin to return to its normal temperature. As you take action, you may notice a decrease in body temperature, reduced sweating, and a general feeling of calmness. Your energy levels may also start to increase, and you may feel more comfortable and relaxed. In some cases, you may even be able to return to your normal activities sooner than expected. However, if your chills persist or worsen, it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for chills

intro_desc: When you're pregnant, your body goes through lots of changes. One of these changes can be feeling cold or chilled, even if the room is warm. This can happen because your body is working harder to support the growth of your baby, and your blood vessels are expanding to carry more blood to your baby. As a result, you might feel cold or chilly, especially in your hands and feet. This can be uncomfortable and even affect your daily life, making it hard to sleep, work, or even do simple tasks. Some pregnant women might even feel anxious or worried about their health when they feel chilled, which can make the situation even more challenging.  Pregnant women who experience chills might also be more likely to get infections or have other health problems, like low blood pressure or a weakened immune system. This is because their body is already under stress from the pregnancy, and feeling chilled can make things worse. On top of this, some women might be taking medications that can cause side effects like dizziness or fatigue, which can make them feel even more chilled.  Feeling chilled can also affect a pregnant woman's mood and energy levels. Some women might feel tired or irritable, which can make it hard to take care of themselves and their baby. They might also have trouble sleeping or concentrating, which can make everyday tasks feel even more overwhelming.  When a pregnant woman feels chilled, she might try to warm up with a blanket or a hot bath. But sometimes, these remedies don't work, or they might not be comfortable or safe to use while pregnant. This is where herbal teas come in - they can be a natural and soothing way to help a pregnant woman feel better when she's chilled. Herbal teas are made from plants that have been used for centuries to help with all sorts of health issues, including fever and chills. They can help to relax the body and mind, making it easier to fall asleep or feel more comfortable when you're chilled.
